programme.title: Foreign Correspondent
programme.desc: From South America to the Solomon Islands, Foreign Correspondent reveals tactics used by cartels to send drugs across the Pacific. The hunt for \.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: Guest Presenter Jeremy Fernandez joins the Sydney Youth Orchestras on an unforgettable musical journey to outback Wilcannia, where they'll perform with local singers, musicians and form new friendships.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Ros Childs presents the leading news at lunchtime, including the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: National Press Club Address
programme.sub-title: Mark Butler - Minister for Health, Ageing, Disability and the NDIS
programme.desc: Mark Butler, Minister for Health and Ageing, and the Minister for Disability and the National Disability Insurance Scheme, addresses the National Press Club.

programme.title: Media Watch
programme.desc: Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.

programme.title: Anzac Battlefields: The Western Front
programme.desc: It is 1917 and the ANZACS are involved in the seminal battles of Bullecourt, Ypres, Messines and Menin Road.

programme.title: Anzac Battlefields: The Western Front
programme.desc: We walk where the battalions fought and where the artillery sank in liquid mud in one of the most notorious killing fields of WWI - Passchendaele.

programme.title: Grand Designs Transformations
programme.desc: With two transformations on one site, builder Dylan tries to retrofit a draughty cottage into a fully sustainable home while landscape architect Tylah's plans for a garden oasis are battered with financial constraints.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch puts his career on the line when he investigates his police brethren after a constable is found murdered in Chinatown.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Revisits the siblings Vanda and Davina as they celebrate sister Debbie's birthday; a catch-up with Chris and Sue, who were searching for the son they had put up for adoption as teenagers.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts return for a second visit to Hopetoun House in Queensferry, Scotland, where the finds include Victorian dresses worn by royalty and a 16th century silver jewel box.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.desc: Vying for Tom Gleeson's Big Brass Mug are experts in typefaces, the Tasmanian devil, war criminal Breaker Morant and Aussie songbird Tina Arena.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News NSW brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Jeremy Fernandez, plus weather with Tom Saunders.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Tonight At The Museum
programme.desc: When the doors shut, the comedy begins: Alex Lee is joined by Colin Lane, Lizzy Hoo, Dane Simpson and Katie Robertson chasing display glory.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: In the season finale of The Weekly, Charlie is joined by the stars of the new ABC comedy Bad Company, Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds, plus Rhys Nicholson, Margaret Pomeranz and Alexei Toliopoulos.

programme.title: Can You Keep A Secret?
programme.desc: Not only was the doctor a bit hasty in pronouncing Pamela's death - but Neha discovers that Pamela's heart attack wasn't Debbie's fault either. She was being poisoned! Maybe she isn't the blackmailer after all?

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Join Sandi Toksvig for a Wet and Windy episode of QI with Chris McCausland, Zoe Lyons, Ed Byrne and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Planet America
programme.desc: Chas and John discuss the latest from the Middle East, Donald Trump's fight with the Pope, questions over the President's cognitive health, the State Visit by King Charles from April 27-30, plus the events of the weekend.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Jade Barker in Perth.

programme.title: The Business
programme.desc: Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, employees and business owners big and small. Presented by Kirsten Aiken.

programme.title: You Can't Ask That
programme.desc: An emotional episode on postnatal depression, where eight parents explore the shame, public and family pressure, plus the personal guilt that surrounds this often misunderstood and taboo topic.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch puts his career on the line when he investigates his police brethren after a constable is found murdered in Chinatown.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Revisits the siblings Vanda and Davina as they celebrate sister Debbie's birthday; a catch-up with Chris and Sue, who were searching for the son they had put up for adoption as teenagers.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Pruning Citrus, Historic Garden & Berry Pot
programme.desc: Costa Georgiadis visits a beautiful, productive rental garden; Millie Ross explores a cool-climate display garden; Jane Edmanson finds green space in the city; guest presenter Luke Mitchell fixes outdoor furniture.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Alice takes a trip to the Torres Strait Islands with chef Nornie Bero. Alice makes banana bread out of bits and bobs from the pantry. And Nornie champions native ingredients in a seafood dish from her childhood.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast
programme.desc: Start your Thursday with all the news you need. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest overnight news, interviews and the top stories this morning, plus Catherine Murphy with sport and Nate Byrne with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

programme.title: Tonight At The Museum
programme.desc: When the doors shut, the comedy begins: Alex Lee is joined by Colin Lane, Lizzy Hoo, Dane Simpson and Katie Robertson chasing display glory.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: In the season finale of The Weekly, Charlie is joined by the stars of the new ABC comedy Bad Company, Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds, plus Rhys Nicholson, Margaret Pomeranz and Alexei Toliopoulos.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Join Sandi Toksvig for a Wet and Windy episode of QI with Chris McCausland, Zoe Lyons, Ed Byrne and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Can You Keep A Secret?
programme.desc: Not only was the doctor a bit hasty in pronouncing Pamela's death - but Neha discovers that Pamela's heart attack wasn't Debbie's fault either. She was being poisoned! Maybe she isn't the blackmailer after all?

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: The leading news at lunchtime. Melissa Mackay brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Frauds
programme.desc: The day of the heist. With tensions high, bitter resentment and revenge in the air, can Sam and Bert keep it together long enough to pull it off and escape each other forever?

programme.title: Anzac Battlefields: The Western Front
programme.desc: The Germans launched the massive Operation Michael on an 80 km front on March 21st 1918, the greatest offensive of the war.

programme.title: Anzac Battlefields: The Western Front
programme.desc: Monash takes up the challenge of vigorous pursuit of the Germans and in one day advances 10 kms and takes two and a half thousand prisoners - one week later the war is over.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: New host of Grand Designs Australia Anthony Burke is joined by Grand Designs UK icon Kevin McCloud on a road trip to see the best contemporary Australian architecture.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface investigates a shocking murder at a groovy pirate radio station.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: A catch-up with Peter, whose joy at his reunion with his birth mother Daphne was short-lived due to a health scare; in Barbados, Jade sees her father Tony for the first time since they were reunited.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team set up camp for another busy day as thousands of visitors bring their own treasures to the forecourt of the British Museum in London.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.desc: Tom Gleeson dishes out the points and the put-downs as four experts vie for the Big Brass Mug with their topics: tennis star Samantha Stosur, Queen singer Freddie Mercury, TV sitcom Seinfeld, and the Galapagos tortoise.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News NSW brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Jeremy Fernandez, plus weather with Tom Saunders.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: Lisa Millar explores Australia's Jurassic Park. Mission Beach is a tropical paradise in Queensland where two World Heritage natural wonders meet, home to the Southern Cassowary.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Sarah and Pip are smitten with a 150-year barn. They are determined to keep its spirit and transform it into a sleek and modern 5-bedroom home. It's a huge challenge.

programme.title: The Piano
programme.desc: It's concert time for the five chosen pianists, where they'll be headlining an extraordinary concert at the prestigious City Recital Hall in Sydney.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Jade Barker in Perth.

programme.title: The Business
programme.desc: Alicia Barry presents Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing viewers up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, investors, employees, and businesses big and small.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface investigates a shocking murder at a groovy pirate radio station.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: A catch-up with Peter, whose joy at his reunion with his birth mother Daphne was short-lived due to a health scare; in Barbados, Jade sees her father Tony for the first time since they were reunited.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Wendy Whiteley, Chook Coop Tips & Microfarms
programme.desc: Sophie Thomson visits an Adelaide garden with a tropical feel; Tino Carnevale meets a microfarmer getting big results; Josh Byrne prunes native plants; Jane Edmanson explores the edible public gardens of Dandenong.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Alice cooks soupy hot potatoes for purple Wiggle, John Pearce. Alice and John go all in with one of John's favourite Filipino ingredients, snake beans. And Alice finds out what makes John so big and strong veggies!

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport, and Nate Byrne with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: In her brief residency in East Sydney,A theA sculptureA of mythical sex worker Joy was both celebrated and attacked. Three decades later, a group of supporters are on a missionA to bring Joy home.

programme.title: Stuff the British Stole
programme.desc: Stephen Fry joins Marc Fennell on a gripping adventure, from the pinnacle of the Acropolis to a secluded robotics lab high in the Tuscan mountains to a shipwreck under the Aegean Sea.

programme.title: Blue Murder Motel
programme.desc: Mowai Bay is in the path of a ferocious summer storm, forcing stranded holidaymakers to bunk down in the Blue Motel conference room. Vinny and Cole investigate the death of a local radio DJ.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Ros Childs presents the leading news at lunchtime, including the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.
